title: Chrysolaena campestris
taxonomy: Chrysolaena campestris is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to Brazil and is often found in dry, open areas. The classification of this plant is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Chrysolaena, Species: C. campestris. This plant has been classified based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup. The classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships between different plant species.

intro: Chrysolaena campestris, also known as a type of flowering plant, is a species of the daisy-like Asteraceae family native to Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Chrysolaena campestris include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
